what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the file-discovered GUIDE registry: it defines what a guide is, its required schema, the mandatory grounding rule, how guides are discovered from guides/*.py, how guide://<id> resolves, and it lists the live guide entries.
observations: Guides are narrative how-to documents and the second face of skills: skill:// is the dense instruction unit, guide:// is the learner-facing narrative, both projections of a target.
A guide is declared as a module-level GUIDE dict with required id, content, target, and grounded_from, plus optional source_hash, label, and description.
grounded_from must be non-empty; an ungrounded guide is not allowed.
The carrier rule says guides attach to non-obvious-use systems, capabilities, or domains, not to simple operational role inputs.
Freshness is tracked by a source_hash of grounded_from at authoring time; a hash delta marks the guide stale and triggers re-authoring via proposed diff, not auto-clobber.
The live set currently includes using_skills, using_patterned_visibility, and using_corpus_pipeline.
Guides are discovered by runtime/guides.py:GuideRegistry reading guides/*.py and resolved by runtime/cognition.py:resolve_address dispatching guide:// to GuideRegistry.read(id).
An unknown guide id raises a fail-loud error; the registry is treated as truth.
This file is the drift home: adding a guide requires updating this constitution, and tests/guides_acceptance.py fails if a discovered guide is not reflected here.
Agent-authored entries are appended as one-line auto-reflected items.
standouts: The one entry, two faces design: skill:// and guide:// are separate addressable projections of the same target, deliberately not co-located.
Mandatory-grounding gate: a guide must be authored from real source addresses or it does not exist.
This constitution file doubles as the acceptance-tested drift home for the live guide set.
conventions: Uses constitution frontmatter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Mirrors the SkillRegistry mechanism in runtime/guides.py rather than inventing a second registry.
Declares fail-loud behavior for unknown ids and ungrounded guides.
Requires the guide id to equal the file stem and the live set to be reflected in this drift home.
References related constitutions and runtime files with [[...]] and scheme addresses.
concerns: The agent-authored entries section mixes an HTML comment instruction with live entries; formatting could drift.
The live set is represented in both the prose list and the agent-authored entries list, creating a potential synchronization hazard.
Freshness enforcement depends on manual re-authoring on source_hash deltas; no automatic stale detection or blocking is described here.
notes: This is the constitution and drift-home for the guide:// registry: read it to learn how narrative guides are declared, grounded, discovered, resolved, and kept in sync.
questions: What is the exact file format of guides/<id>.py beyond a top-level GUIDE dict, and how is multi-line content stored?
Which hash algorithm is used for source_hash and how are multiple grounded_from sources combined?
What is the declarative-direct face that produced the agent-authored entries?
How does tests/guides_acceptance.py parse this markdown to verify every discovered guide is reflected?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and actual content align: it defines the guide registry, schema, resolution path, freshness practice, and canonical live guide list.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth and one-home rule
intention_signals: It aims to make narrative how-to guides first-class, addressable, automatically discoverable artifacts that are always tied to real system sources and kept consistent through a constitution-backed registry and acceptance tests.
novelty: It treats narrative guides as addressable registry entries with mandatory source grounding, content hashes for freshness, and a constitutional drift-home file that is acceptance-tested.
contribution: It provides the conceptual contract and live catalog for guide:// entries, ensuring guides are grounded in real sources, discoverable from files, resolvable through the address seam, and synchronized with the file set.
summary_for_embedding: The module constitution for the company's file-discovered guide registry, defining the GUIDE schema, mandatory grounding, discovery and resolution via guide://, freshness via source_hash, and the canonical list of live guides.
intention_for_embedding: To make narrative how-to guides addressable, grounded in real sources, discoverable from files, and kept consistent through a constitution-backed registry and acceptance tests.
